communications.communication:

* By "sent to," I also mean emails CC'd or BCC'd to the people named.

You may limit the search to the dates between 1 January 2026 and the date this request is processed.

If any emails contain attachments, please include those attachments as well. If some portions of the requested records are properly exempt from disclosure, please disclose any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions of the requested records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

title: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All emails between Travis Johnson, Chief of Staff to Attorney General Chris Carr, and Frank Russo (also known as Frank W. Russo Jr.), Partner/Vice President of Modern Fortis, from January 1, 2025 through the date of this request.

"frank@modernfortis.com" <frank@modernfortis.com>

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Georgia Open Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on May 14, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication: This acknowledges receipt of your request under the Open Records Act and provides our required initial response.  Our office requires additional time to search and review our records to response to this request.  We anticipate that our initial review will take at least ten business days.  Responsive records, if any, may contain materials that are exempt from disclosure under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72 or other applicable law including, but not limited to, materials that are exempt as work product or attorney-client privileged.    We anticipate that our charge to comply with your request will exceed $100.00.   Please advise whether you will be responsible for this cost.  Please also advise as to the status of your payment of prior amounts incurred.   We anticipate providing our best time and cost estimate for completing your request in the next 10 business days.  We reserve the right to provide a revision as our review progresses.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication:
Thank you for your acknowledgment. Please note that I have mailed checks for any previously required payments.

I write to respectfully push back on the anticipated cost estimate exceeding $100. This request is narrowly scoped to emails between two specific individuals — Travis Johnson and Frank Russo at frank@modernfortis.com — over a defined 16-month period. A simple email search by sender and recipient should require minimal staff time and no extraordinary resources. I would ask that you provide a specific, itemized cost estimate as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71 before proceeding.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication:
We have completed our search and review.  The cost of processing your request is $44.15, which includes 0.67 hours of administrative time at a rate of $20 per hour and 0.50 hours of attorney management time at a rate of $61.50 per hour.

We redacted records containing cell phone numbers under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(a)(20).

If you wish to proceed, please confirm in writing that you will be responsible for payment of the costs of response.


Cordially,

Open Records
Office of the Attorney General
Georgia Department of Law
40 Capitol Square SW
Atlanta, Georgia 30334

communications.communication:
Thank you for your email.   We have found five relevant email records for production.  The cost of records prodcution is $5.00, which includes .25 hours of administrative time at a rate of $20 per hour.

If you wish to proceed, please confirm in writing that you will be responsible for payment of the costs of response.  Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication:
Thank you for the updated estimate. 

However, the entire billable time — 0.25 hours (15 minutes) of administrative time — falls within the mandatory free period under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(c), which requires the first 15 minutes of search, retrieval, and redaction time to be provided at no charge. There is no remaining billable time. Please produce the five responsive records at no cost.

Jason Hunyar

title: Law enforcement informal and formal settlement data and reports (Sparks Police Department)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Law enforcement informal and formal settlement data and reports (Sparks Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Materials related to and sufficient to show all instances of alleged or actual inappropriate or illegal behavior or actions resulting in compensation or expenditures involving this agency. 

Responsive materials include records of all settlements (formal and informal) and payments to which this agency was or is a party for the period from January 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed. Responsive materials include those for matters and legal claims resolved prior to the initiation of any litigation. 

In particular, materials responsive to this request include all reports, lists, databases, spreadsheets, and other documentation detailing incidents of legal claims or other allegations requiring compensation for claimants, attorneys, or any other party. 

If spreadsheets or similar documents are available in response to this request, please provide all segregable data, including but not limited to:

The nature of the claim or legal matter
The claimant and other parties involved
The relevant case and tracking numbers for court and internal systems
The date the lawsuit was served or litigation commenced
The final disposition or current status of the case, including whether a case is still pending
The amount spent to date as a result of the legal engagement
The amount paid to any parties related to the legal claim, including any city payout and any non-monetary compensation

If clarification of this request is required, please respond to the provided email address or leave a message at 617-299-1832.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Annemarie Grant

communications.communication:
Good day, 
 
Your request: 20261098 - LAW ENFORCEMENT INFORMAL AND FORMAL SETTLEMENT DATA AND REPORTS (SPARKS POLICE DEPARTMENT) is now completed. 
 
 The record(s) you have been provided do not contain any documentation from other agencies or any confidential information.  Sparks Police Department  is required to redact or remove all personal identifying information (NRS 603A.040), proprietary information (NRS 333.335), information from the National Crime Information Center (28 CFR § 50.12(b); USC 28 § 534(f)(1); CJIS Security Policy Section 4.2.3), information about ongoing criminal investigations (NRS 199.520; Donrey of Nevada v. Bradshaw, 106 Nev. 630, 798 P.2d 144 (1990) ), information about victims of crimes (Nevada Constitution, Article 1, Section 23; NRS 178.5691); and information relating to individuals’ private interests such that disclosure to the public would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of privacy (Clark Cnty. Sch. Dist. v. Las Vegas Review-Journal, 134 Nev. 700, 429 P.3d 313 (2018)). 
 
 If your request contained any of the above information, it has been redacted or removed as required by law. 
 
Electronic records are available here for the next 60 days: 
 
 
If you receive “Invalid Source” when attempting to view a document, download the document and open it in a separate window. 
 
For non-electronic records, please refer to the attached Record Summary to schedule an appointment to review the records. 
 
Best regards.

title: County and Municipal Budgets

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: County and Municipal Budgets
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All county and municipal budgets from year 2012-2013 to 2025-2026 for each county and municipal government as listed on the State Auditor’s website at https://www.wvsao.gov/LocalGovernment/Default. I’m requesting this data because there is not a bulk download button on the website, and the process of gathering and sending the data to me should be more simple for you than for me to individually download or scrape your website. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. On the State Auditor’s website, these files are provided as PDFs. However, I suspect that the raw data is stored in a tabular data format like spreadsheets or a relational database. If this the case, please note that I am requesting the tabular or native digital format and not the PDF reports. If there you are not in possession of any responsive records other than the PDFs, then I am requesting the PDFs themselves. 

If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: 1st  Response to FOIA Request dated May 26, 2026

Barbara L. Wolfe

title: Miami MFA emails (House 2026; selected via Grok)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Miami MFA emails (House 2026; selected via Grok)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Emails sent to or from* any of the officials (and/or their staffers) listed below and any email address ending with mfa.gov.il or gov.il:

•  Daniel Perez
•  Tyler Sirois 
•  Chuck Brannan
•  Hillary Cassel
•  Yvette Benarroch
•  Mike Caruso
•  Peggy Gossett-Seidman
•  Chip LaMarca
•  Michelle Salzman
•  Debra Tendrich
•  Mitch Rosenwald
•  Kelly Skidmore
•  Allison Tant
•  Katherine Waldron
•  Jeff Holcomb
•  Michael Gottlieb
•  Jennifer Canady
•  Vicki Lopez
•  Robbie Brackett
•  Tiffany Esposito

* By "sent to," I also mean emails CC'd or BCC'd to the people named.

You may limit the search to the dates between 1 January 2026 and the date this request is processed.

If any emails contain attachments, please include those attachments as well. If some portions of the requested records are properly exempt from disclosure, please disclose any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions of the requested records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: PRR 198 Matthew Petti (Speaker Perez, et al. - mfa.gov.il)
communications.communication:
The Office of Open Government received the request below.  Records maintained by the House on the House server will be searched for public records that meet the criteria of the request.  Any such records will be provided to you in accordance with Article 1, Section 24(c) of the Florida Constitution; section 11.0431, Florida Statutes; and House Rules 14.1 and 14.2.

For your information, requests for public records that are maintained by a member, including text messages, social media posts, paper records, and emails to or from personal email accounts, must be submitted directly to the member.

Kind regards,

Office of Open Government
Florida House of Representatives

Please Note: The Florida Constitution requires disclosure of public records unless a Florida Statute exempts the records from the disclosure requirement.  Most emails to and from House members and staff that were sent or received in connection with the transaction of legislative business are public records that will be made available to the public and media upon request.  Therefore, the contents of your email and your email address are subject to public disclosure unless a specific statute exempts them from the Constitution’s disclosure requirements.

communications.subject: PRR 198 Matthew Petti (Speaker Perez, et al. - mfa.gov.il)
communications.communication:
In response to the request below, records that are maintained by the House were searched for public records that meet the criteria of the request.  Those searches yielded a number of public records, which are being provided via the House’s FTP due to file size.  You can expect an email from the House’s FTP site with the link to the records within minutes of receiving this email.

Please note that the link contained within the FTP email will expire in two weeks.

If found, electronic newsletters received by House employees have been omitted.  If you desire copies, please advise within the next five business days.  Published bills and bill analyses are publicly available at www.myfloridahouse.gov<http://www.myfloridahouse.gov>.

The House considers this matter closed.

Kind regards,

Office of Open Government
Florida House of Representatives

Please Note: The Florida Constitution requires disclosure of public records unless a Florida Statute exempts the records from the disclosure requirement.  Most emails to and from House members and staff that were sent or received in connection with the transaction of legislative business are public records that will be made available to the public and media upon request.  Therefore, the contents of your email and your email address are subject to public disclosure unless a specific statute exempts them from the Constitution’s disclosure requirements.

title: Request to Department of Defense for 2025 budget of the Office of the Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Department of Defense for 2025 budget of the Office of the Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The 2025 annual budget for the Office of the Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: William M. Beecher (May 27, 1933 – February 9, 2024)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: William M. Beecher (May 27, 1933 – February 9, 2024)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into investigative journalist William M. Beecher (May 27, 1933 – February 9, 2024). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2024/02/18/business/media/william-beecher-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an investigative journalist who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, Reason Magazine, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

    of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the
    request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM
    if not.
    
    Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look
    forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days,
    as the statute requires.
    
    Sincerely,
    
    Matthew Petti

Supporting documentation **

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBIâ€™s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBIâ€™s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the â€˜Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request toolâ€™ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1620220-000
communications.communication:
To whom this may concern,

My name is Matthew Petti and I am writing in response to my FOIA request number 1620220-000. Thank you so much for your prompt response.

In your letter dated 6 March 2024, you write that "additional records potentially responsive to your subject may exist. If this release of previously processed material does not satisfy your request, you may request an additional search for records."

I would like to formally advise that I would like an additional search for records. Thank you again for your prompt attention to this matter. If possible, please let me know if you've received this message.

Best regards,
Matthew

communications.subject: FOIA Request 1620220-001/William Beecher
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

We're contacting you in reference to the FOIPA request number included in the subject line of this email.  The FBI located approximately 2,083 pages potentially responsive to your request. 

Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system:
Extra-small track requests (0-50 pages) current average time is approximately 8 months to complete;
Small track requests (51-250 pages) current average time is approximately 36 months to complete;
Medium track requests (251-500 pages) current average time is approximately 44 months to complete;
Large track requests (501-4,999 pages) current average time is approximately 67 months to complete; and
Extra-large track requests (5,000+ pages) current average time is approximately 79 months to complete.

The current average time to complete your large track request is at least 67 months. Reducing the scope of your request may accelerate the processing, allow for a timelier receipt of the information you seek, and reduce the duplication costs, if applicable.  Would you be willing to consider reducing the scope of your request to place it in a smaller, potentially faster processing track?

Please let us know if this is something you would be interested in further discussing. If you chose to narrow the scope of your request, you will be able to request remaining material via a new FOIPA request submission at the completion of this request, subject to applicable fees.

Respectfully,

Negotiation Team
Federal Bureau of Investigation
Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov<mailto:Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov>

Status Checks: https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__vault.fbi.gov_fdps-2D1_-40-40search-2Dfdps&d=DwMFAw&c=sJ6xIWYx-zLMB3EPkvcnVg&r=XBMMkObpUV4qgiRWe16ruf84MlE_zzfeACVcd84hmls&m=Aw62oYIKH07oNuehHecdHgLWMqlWFG0DYoQjNpzMHkhbi3BtoiHrci3yBRh212Wp&s=SdHEKbKopxbZbSeRluCcd86nmwJXks1SzF_CE3LxaTE&e=> | foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>
For additional information, please visit the www.fbi.gov/foia<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__www.fbi.gov_foia&d=DwMFAw&c=sJ6xIWYx-zLMB3EPkvcnVg&r=XBMMkObpUV4qgiRWe16ruf84MlE_zzfeACVcd84hmls&m=Aw62oYIKH07oNuehHecdHgLWMqlWFG0DYoQjNpzMHkhbi3BtoiHrci3yBRh212Wp&s=-aTlmvcM5Bk6Q7nyu9yDBhN3v2HmU3sGIZ7u9r2JLFg&e=> website.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1620220-001
communications.communication:
Dear Negotiation Team,

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Matthew Petti and I am writing with regard to my FOIA request number 1620220-001/William Beecher. Do you have any suggestions on how to reduce the scope of the request? Otherwise, I am happy to leave it in the large track.

Sincerely,
Matthew

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1620220-001
communications.communication:
Good morning,
Thank you for your email concerning the scope reduction of this request.  Unfortunately, we are limited on what we can discuss since the potentially responsive pages haven’t been reviewed by a FOIA analyst.
After further review, the potentially responsive pages are within the date range of 1953-1987.
We recommend the following options:

We could narrow the scope of this request by limiting to a specific date range within 1953-1987.

We could reduce the scope of this request by limiting to approximately 50 potentially responsive pages.  This request would move to the simple track with an average time to complete of approximately 8 months.

We could reduce the scope of this request by limiting to approximately 250 potentially responsive pages.  This request would move to the small track with an average time to complete of approximately 36 months.

We could reduce the scope of this request by limiting to approximately 500 potentially responsive pages.  This request would move to the medium track with an average time to complete of approximately 44 months.

Please let us know how you would like to move forward with reducing the scope of your request.
Respectfully,

Negotiation Team
Federal Bureau of Investigation
Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov<mailto:Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov>

Status Checks: https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__vault.fbi.gov_fdps-2D1_-40-40search-2Dfdps&d=DwMFAw&c=sJ6xIWYx-zLMB3EPkvcnVg&r=XBMMkObpUV4qgiRWe16ruf84MlE_zzfeACVcd84hmls&m=Aw62oYIKH07oNuehHecdHgLWMqlWFG0DYoQjNpzMHkhbi3BtoiHrci3yBRh212Wp&s=SdHEKbKopxbZbSeRluCcd86nmwJXks1SzF_CE3LxaTE&e=> | foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

title: DDA-75-5800 / GAO Audit Task Force final report

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: DDA-75-5800 / GAO Audit Task Force final report
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of DDA-75-5800, the 5 December 1975 final report of the GAO Audit Task Force.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

title: HPSCI Report on the Implementation of FISA

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: HPSCI Report on the Implementation of FISA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the HPSCI Report on the Implementation of FISA described in CIA-RDP86B00338R000200330012-3.

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers in outlets such as MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, AND Magazine, Unicorn Riot, and more. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive and and the journalist non-profit, MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.

Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category.

As my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it and making the raw data available, I request that fees be waived.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best
An acknowledgement letter, stating the request is being processed.

title: William A Read

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: William A Read
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any records pertaining to William August Read (born August 21, 1895) and his employment by or work for the Central Intelligence Agency or associated projects. He is my great grandfather and there are family stories about his work for the CIA, but I would be very curious around any records of it.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Benjamin Read

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2025 H1 IAPro data
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request the listing of OPA allegations in IAPro with a received date on or after January 1, 2025 with columns:
 Occurred date    OPA #    CAD/Incident No    ID number    First name    Last name    Date hired    Incident type    Directive    Finding    Finding date    Action taken    Employee Type 
I request the listing be provided in Excel format so I can easily sort/filter it. Redactions should be done in Excel.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request. 
 SPD strongly encourages online payments. 
 
To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Find this request: P255844-020826. 
  * Select the 'View Invoices' button. 
 
  * Opens a pop-up window showing your invoice: INV26-P255844-1. 
  * Click on the invoice and then the 'Pay Now' button. 
  * Continue processing your payment. 
  * Select 'Done'. 
  * Once the payment process is done, you are returned to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * After your payment is successful you will receive an email from our payments center. After we receive your payment through the Police Records Request Center Secure Payments, the Public Disclosure Unit will release your records within one to two business days and send you a notice with instructions on how to download the records.

title: Unredacted contract 3640 between City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Unredacted contract 3640 between City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

An unredacted, complete copy of City of Seattle Supplier Contract #000000000003640 (and all associated change orders) between the City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise, Inc., originally dated on or about November 5, 2024.

To clarify, this request specifically seeks the full, unredacted content of the following sections which were redacted in previous disclosures:
 - Attachment C – Pricing
 - Attachment D – Evidence.com Terms of Use
 - Section 1.26 of Attachment A, Statement of Work (SOW)
 - All Service Level Agreements (SLA) or supplemental terms incorporated by reference into [that] agreement."

Please note:
 - Pricing is Public Post-Award: Under Washington law, pricing for "goods and services" purchased with public funds is not a trade secret under RCW 42.56.270 once a contract has been awarded and executed. The public has a paramount interest in knowing the financial terms of surveillance technology procurement.
 - Terms of Use are Not Exempt: Terms of Use (Attachment D) are not proprietary business methods; they are the legal rules governing the Agency’s use of public data. Under City of Sedro-Woolley v. Rodriguez (2025), data "paid for by" and "generated for the benefit of" the City is a public record regardless of storage location or vendor-imposed terms.
 - Prohibition on Contractual Confidentiality: An agency cannot "contract away" its statutory obligations under the PRA. Any clause in the Axon contract promising confidentiality for non-exempt information is void as a matter of public policy.
 - Narrow Construction of Exemptions: Per RCW 42.56.030, the PRA must be liberally construed in favor of disclosure and exemptions must be interpreted narrowly. The City bears the burden of proving that every redacted sentence—including "Terms of Use"—meets a specific statutory exemption.
 - Constructive Possession: Under the Nissen and O'Neill frameworks, the City maintains "constructive possession" of all terms and data within the Axon/Evidence.com ecosystem. Redacting the terms that define this possession (the SOW and Terms of Use) is a violation of the "fullest assistance" mandate in RCW 42.56.100.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

- Terms of Use are Not Exempt: Terms of Use (Attachment D) are not proprietary business methods; they are the legal rules governing the Agency’s use of public data. Under City of Sedro-Woolley v. Rodriguez (2025), data "paid for by" and "generated for the benefit of" the City is a public record regardless of storage location or vendor-imposed terms.

- Constructive Possession: Under the Nissen and O'Neill frameworks, the City maintains "constructive possession" of all terms and data within the Axon/Evidence.com ecosystem. To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 Please note that you will need to disable any pop-up blockers to make a payment using the system. 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center.
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Find this request: C263311-041226.

  * Click on the invoice and then the 'Pay Now' button. 
  * Continue processing your payment. 
  * Select 'Done'. 
  * Once the payment process is done, you are returned to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * After your payment is successful you will receive an email from our payments center. 
Once your payment has been approved, we will send you an email with instructions on how to download your records. Note that City staff must manually upload records. Records will be available one to two business days after payment. 
 
Please let your public disclosure officer (PDO) know if you are unable to pay online; your PDO will work with you on alternative ways to make your payment. 
 
You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request.

Dear Phil Mocek:

title: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Yakima Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Yakima Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

## PRESERVATION DEMAND ##

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt.

Please immediately:

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval.

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation.  Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ##

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production.

## FEE REQUEST ##

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

## RECORDS REQUESTED ##

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals.

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced.

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request:

	Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles
	(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered
	but vehicles are believed to remain in service):
	c. 50816D  — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010)
	d. 50887D  — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031)
	e. 54180D  — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031)
	f. 43500D  — Bomb squad trailer (2005)
	g. 26073D  — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997)
	h. 41461D  — Communications van (2005)
	i. 44372D  — Command vehicle (2005)
	j. 54148D  — Riot fencing trailer (2011)
	k. 54362D  — CSI van with generator (2012)
	l. 50295D  — SWAT cutaway van (2008)
	m. 54117D  — CSI van (2011)
	n. 24208D  — Weapons vehicle (1987)
	
	Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern):
	o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP"
	p. All plates with the prefix "XMT"
	
	Other:
	a. BCH-478
	b. 826-ZVA

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #2108125
communications.communication:
Dear Yakima Police Department,

Please confirm that as I requested on March 11, 2026, you 1) suspended any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds and/or held a contractual right of access or retrieval, and 2) issued a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

I received your unsigned message of April 20.  It appears to me that you simply "waited out the clock" on implementation of SB 6002, the Driver Privacy Act, in order to avoid providing public access to these public records.  I requested records on March 11, weeks before governor Ferguson signed the bill into law. ALPR scan records should be available via a simple database export that takes minutes to accomplish.  There is no doubt after Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley that those records are public.

In your message, you stated that, "all ALPR data the City currently possesses that does not qualify for retention under SB 6002 will be deleted as required by law."  I request that you refrain from destruction of evidence.

RCW 42.56.100 requires that "[i]f a public record request is made at a time when such record exists but is scheduled for destruction in the near future, the agency, the office of the secretary of the senate, or the office of the chief clerk of the house of representatives shall retain possession of the record, and may not destroy or erase the record until the request is resolved."  WAC 44-14-03005 states, "An agency is prohibited from destroying a public record, even if it is about to be lawfully destroyed under a retention schedule, if a public records request has been made for that record."

WAC 44-14-03005 also states, " Additional retention requirements might apply if the records may be relevant to actual or anticipated litigation. The agency is required to retain the record until the record request has been resolved."  I trust that you will maintain ALPR data until at least one year after resolution of any and all requests for those records are closed, when the statute of limitations on PRA violations runs out, potentially ending the potential for litigation that I trust you currently anticipate.

Cordially,
Phil Mocek

At 4:17 p.m. a YPD staff member entered a note into the RecordsApp Portal stating: Made Kodex request #(Flock-391) in response to your request to: 1) suspended any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your
agency holds and/or held a contractual right of access or retrieval, and 2) issued a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution. Correspondence to you on April 20, 2026 is from me, personally, via the RecordsApp portal, which is used to monitor, track and respond to public records requests received by the City of Yakima. As the Records Administrator, I assist the two Public Records Officers as my time allows, in addition to other mandatory duties of my position. The City of Yakima has 281 open public records requests. 127 requests were received prior to yours; 87 of the 127 are considered complex for various reasons. Your request is considered complex because it requires obtaining records from multiple third-party vendors. For information
regarding complex requests and how the City responds to requests for public records, please refer to the City of Yakima’s Public Records Act Procedures: City-of-Yakima-Public-Records-Act-Procedures-08-2024.pdf (https://www.yakimawa.gov/services/clerks/files/2024/09/City-of-Yakima-Public-Records-Act-Procedures-08-2024.pdf) Currently, your request remains open while staff continue to gather and review records responsive to your request and determine if the records are subject to public disclosure. For information regarding complex requests please refer to the City of Yakima’s Public Records Act Procedures: City-of-Yakima-Public-Records-Act-Procedures-08-2024.pdf (https://www.yakimawa.gov/services/clerks/files/2024/09/City-of-Yakima-Public-Records-Act-Procedures-08-2024.pdf) Based on my current workload, the volume and complexity of open public requests, the complexity of this request including records which contain information not subject to the Public Records Act;
other mandatory duties required by statute and my job description including LEOFF1 pension duties, supervision of and coverage for staff during scheduled and unscheduled leave, personal leave, and scheduled and emergent Family Medical Leave; and, pursuant to RCW 42.56.520, additional time is necessary to review responsive records. I anticipate providing an update on the status of your request on or near June 18, 2026 , or sooner if time allows. Thank you. 

 
View it at https://records.yakimawa.gov/record-requests/view/2108125 (https://records.yakimawa.gov/record-requests/view/2108125)

title: Public Records Request — Minnesota Department of Health Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Public Records Request — Minnesota Department of Health Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 24, 2026

Data Practices Compliance Official
Minnesota Department of Health
St. Paul, MN

Dear Data Practices Compliance Official,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Minnesota Department of Health's use of generative AI tools by agency employees.

We are requesting the following records under Minn. Stat. § 13.03, subd. 3:

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot Chat, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by MDH employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from April 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing MDH employee use of Microsoft Copilot Chat or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The Minnesota Department of Health oversees public health policy, disease surveillance, health regulation, and licensing. AI tools may be used for policy analysis, regulatory drafting, epidemiological research, and public communications. MNIT confirmed that Copilot usage data is held by each agency individually, and we are directing this request to MDH for its own employees' usage.

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Microsoft Copilot interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, all government data collected, created, received, maintained, or disseminated by a government entity is presumed public unless classified otherwise by statute. Minn. Stat. §§ 13.01, 13.03. Governor Walz's Executive Order 24-03 (2024) directed state agencies to develop policies for responsible AI use including transparency and accountability. Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) rolled out Microsoft Copilot Chat to executive branch employees in April 2025 as part of the state's existing Microsoft contract (see GovTech, https://www.govtech.com/biz/minnesota-taps-free-genai-through-microsoft-partnership). MNIT has confirmed that Copilot usage data for individual agencies is held by each agency, not centrally by MNIT. Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

title: 2024-2025 SFI Filings

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All Statements of Financial Interests filed with the Office of State Ethics for filing years 2024 and 2025, submitted through the date this request is fulfilled. This includes filings by all filers designated by the Governor and under Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-83.

Based on the filing indexes shared at https://www.oseapps.ct.gov/sfi/,  I've attached a spreadsheet identifying the individual filings by name, agency, filing year, filing date, and filing type, in case that may be helpful in processing this request.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: FW: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Nielsen,

This is to acknowledge your Freedom of Information request of February 16, 2026, to the Office of State Ethics (OSE) for copies of all Statements of Financial Interests (SFIs) for a list of 3,175 filer names for calendar years 2024 and 2025.  SFIs are filed annually and are due May 1 for the preceding calendar year.  Therefore, the calendar year 2025 filings will be due May 1, 2026, and we cannot fulfill this portion of your request.  However, we do have all filings for calendar year 2024.  Because your listing contains some duplicate names (persons who may hold a state of Connecticut position and also serve on a board) there are approximately 2,916 individuals currently listed in our database.  This may vary slightly from the number of required filer names as of May 1, 2025, but OSE will provide all existing records responsive to your request.

Although we can attach PDF copies of selected SFIs in satisfaction of smaller Freedom of Information requests using email, the state of Connecticut Information Technology protocol prohibits uploading or interacting with non-state of Connecticut systems.  For larger requests such as yours, the only method we have to complete the request is to save the PDF copies to one or more computer disks.  These can be mailed to a physical address, if you can provide one such that the disks will reach you.  The only cost for this is the replacement cost for the blank media we use, which is $1 per disk.  An invoice will be included with the mailing.  I anticipate that the disk(s) can be prepared no later than February 25, 2026 and mailed out to you on that date, if you can respond to this email with a physical address.

Sincerely,


___________________________
Diane P. Buxo
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division, Office of State Ethics
165 Capitol Avenue
Hartford, CT 06106
Tel: (860) 263-2416 Ofc

Ethics Matters!<http://www.ct.gov/ethics>
[cid:image001.jpg@01DC9FEE.B9233F40]<https://portal.ct.gov/>
LEGAL NOTICE:  Certain communications or records received by or sent from this electronic mail account may be subject to public disclosure pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-200 et seq.  If you have received this in error, please notify us immediately by return email and promptly delete this message and its attachments from your computer system.

communications.subject: RE: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
Good morning Ms. Buxo, 

Thank you so much for your help. Given the constraints for transferring a larger dataset, I'm happy to receive the documents on discs. If it may even be easier, for a larger capacity, I'd be happy to purchase a sealed USB. 

Can you send the disc(s) (or whatever best serves) to: 
Audrey Nielsen 
76 Brush Mill Trl 
Higganum, CT 06441 

Your explanation on the total count makes perfect sense. RE: 2025 filings, it was my understanding from the data summary on the website that ~123 filings for 2025 had been filed ahead of the deadline. It's totally possible I misunderstood the dashboard, though. Would it be possible to arrange a similar production for all 2025 filings filed by deadline in advance, for once they're producible after May 1, 2026? I know this is not an obligation under the FOI act, but in case it might be benefit for allocating time and resources. And of course I would appreciate it. 

I appreciate your time and help. 

Best, 

Audrey

communications.subject: RE: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Nielsen,

Thank you for providing me with a physical address, and I will get the disk or disks out to you for calendar year 2024 filings as soon as possible.

As to the calendar year 2025 filings, some of which may be filed prior to May 1, it would be best (in terms of time expended, since you are requesting all the filings – rather than just those for one agency, for example) that I follow up to have our IT Department prepare a disk or disks for you shortly after May 1, 2026 so that you will get most (if not all) of the filed SFIs.  You are correctly that generally, because OSE is a smaller agency, we ask requestors to follow up with a new FOI request after the annual filing date has passed.  However, in this case I will keep your request for the entire calendar year 2025 filing in a pending file to be worked on shortly after May 1.  There are always a few individuals who didn’t receive a reminder email at their employing agency and who file a bit after the deadline.  There are often a few others who are considered actually delinquent, rather than just late filers, but because there are close to 3,000 separate filings, IT downloads the SFIs in bulk and if there is a particular name of interest to you who isn’t included, you can query about it after you’ve received the disk and looked over its data.

Since I asked you for a physical address, I should note that our office is now located at 165 Capitol Avenue, Suite 1200, Hartford, CT 06106. Our emails and telephone numbers did not change, and my direct extension is noted below.

Sincerely,



___________________________
Diane P. Buxo
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division, Office of State Ethics
165 Capitol Avenue
Hartford, CT 06106
Tel: (860) 263-2416 Ofc

Ethics Matters!<http://www.ct.gov/ethics>
[cid:image001.jpg@01DCA016.F4145490]<https://portal.ct.gov/>

Gen. Stat. § 1-200 et seq.  If you have received this in error, please notify us immediately by return email and promptly delete this message and its attachments from your computer system.

communications.subject: RE: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: 2024-2025 SFI Filings
communications.communication:
Good morning,

Thanks so much for your help with this request! I really appreciate it. I wanted to confirm that I received the filings CD in the mail, was able to access the files, and will send the disc back in the envelope provided. 

I really appreciate your help with the upcoming filings, and the timeline constraints you mentioned make sense to me. 

Sincerely,
Audrey Nielsen

communications.communication:
Good morning Ms. Nielsen,

Thanks so much for confirming that you received the disk.  You don’t need to return it (but can do so if you wish); the replacement cost is $1.00.  OSE does not usually reuse disks, but if you return it I can ask IT to erase those files, if you’ve saved them already, and then I can follow up to use it for the 2025 SFI files when they are available.

Sincerely,


___________________________
Diane P. Buxo

communications.subject: Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Axon and Fusus Contracts | Q2 2026 | DeKalb County Department of Purchasing and Contracting
communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you for your request to review County records.  On May 29, 2026, we received your Open Records Act request for the following documents:

All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Axon and Fusus that were active any time between 1/1/2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.

After reviewing your request, it has been determined that requested documents maintained by Purchasing and Contracting are subject to release under the Open Records Act, except as noted below and is attached.

The County and its officials and departments do not produce documents or records that are protected by the attorney-client privilege or the attorney work product doctrine under Georgia or federal law or that are otherwise exempt from production under the Georgia Open Records Act.  The County expressly reserves and does not waive any statutory exemptions from disclosure provided by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72, et seq. and other applicable laws.


Thank You,

[Home | DeKalb County GA]

title: Citation/Traffic Stop Data Request (Durham Police Department)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Citation/Traffic Stop Data Request (Durham Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

"To whom it may concern,

Please provide the following infomration on traffic citations in the form of  spreadsheets (.xls or csv, machine readable) covering the years 2019 to 2023.

These spreadsheets should include the following feilds:

Date 
Time
Race of the Motorist
Ethnicity of the motorist
Gender of the Motorist 
Motorist First Name
Mostorist Last Name
Motorist Date of Birth
County
Reason For Stop
Enforcement Action
Longitude
Latitude
City
State
ZipCode
CitationNo
VehicleYear
VehicleMake
VehicleModel
ViolationDescription
SpeedActual
SpeedPosted

If there are any issues or confusions about the feilds please feel free to reach out.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Sam

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All superintendent contract documents including the contract itself, any addendums created as well as any other documents that have been created in relation to Superintendent Israel Vela's contract.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Donald Cook

communications.communication:
Dear Donald Cook: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Kent School District. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 5/6/2026 and given the reference number P000157-050626 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All superintendent contract documents including the contract itself, any addendums created as well as any other documents that have been created in relation to Superintendent Israel Vela's contract. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Donald Cook 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2284175/ Video Records Requested: 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
Kent School District

title: Records on Facilitation of Return Police 2018 to Present

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records on Facilitation of Return Police 2018 to Present
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Recently the agency appears to have made extensive changes to the "Return Policy" first announced in Supreme Court's decision in Nken v. Holder. For example, its "Frequently Asked Questions" for this topic (https://www.ice.gov/faq/
facilitating-return) was taken offline in March, 2017 and has since been returned with numerous edits and changes. One of these answers appears to shift responsibility for facilitation of return from the Enforcement and Removal Operations
generally to its "Custody Management Division" specifically. We are concerned with these changes, which have neither been formally announced nor subjected to notice and comment. They appear to indicate both a reduced willingness to
comply with the Solicitor General's reassurances following the Nken decision on the facilitation of return and to indicate that ICE intends to place returned individuals into ICE custody.

Accordingly, I request the following under the Freedom of Information Act:

1. Statistics on facilitation of return requests (also called FoRR Inquiries) from 1/1/09 to present including the following:

a. Number of requests received for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017.
b. Number of requests granted for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017.
c. Number of requests denied for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Hoppock

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Hoppock

as the statute requires.

this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the
request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if
not.

forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days,
as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Hoppock

communications.subject: ICE FOIA Request 2020-ICFO-63645
communications.communication:
July 14, 2020 Matthew Hoppock MuckRock News MuckRock News DEPT MR 98146 411A Highland Ave Somerville, MA 02144 RE:     ICE FOIA Case Number 2020-ICFO-63645  Dear Mr. Hoppock: This acknowledges receipt of your July 13, 2020, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), for records pertaining to changes to the agency's "Return Policy" (see request for details). Your request was received in this office on July 13, 2020. Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Per Section 5.5(a) of the DHS FOIA regulations, 6 C.F.R. Part 5, ICE processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt. Although ICE’s goal is to respond within 20 business days of receipt of your request, the FOIA does permit a 10- day extension of this time period. As your request seeks numerous documents that will necessitate a thorough and wide-ranging search, ICE will invoke a 10-day extension for your request, as allowed by Title 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B). If you care to narrow the scope of your request, please contact our office. We will make every effort to comply with your request in a timely manner. Provisions of the FOIA allow us to recover part of the cost of complying with your request.  We shall charge you for records in accordance with the DHS Interim FOIA regulations, as they apply to media requesters.  As a media requester, you will be charged 10 cents per page for duplication; the first 100 pages are free.  We will construe the submission of your request as an agreement to pay up to $25.00. You will be contacted before any further fees are accrued. We have queried the appropriate program offices within ICE for responsive records. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that one of the processors in our office will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request. 
Your request has been assigned reference number 2020-ICFO-63645. Please refer to this identifier in any future correspondence. To check the status of an ICE FOIA/PA request, please visit . Please note that to check the status of a request, you must enter the 2020-ICFO-63645 tracking number. If you need any further assistance or would like to discuss any aspect of your request, please contact the FOIA office. You may send an e-mail to ice-foia@ice.dhs.gov, call toll free (866) 633-1182, or you may contact our FOIA Public Liaison, Fernando Pineiro, in the same manner. Additionally, you have a right to right to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) which mediates disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation. If you are requesting access to your own records (which is considered a Privacy Act request), you should know that OGIS does not have the authority to handle requests made under the Privacy Act of 1974. You may contact OGIS as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769. (http://www.dhs.gov/foia-status) http://www.dhs.gov/foia-status
Regards, 

ICE FOIA Office 
Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Freedom of Information Act Office 
500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5009 
Telephone: 1-866-633-1182 
Visit our FOIA website at www.ice.gov/foia
