communications.subject: ICE FOIA 2020-ICFO-63645
communications.communication:
12/18/2024 

Matthew Hoppock 
MuckRock News DEPT MR 98146 
Somerville, Massachusetts 02144 

RE:	ICE FOIA Case Number 2020-ICFO-63645 
 
Dear Requester: 

This acknowledges receipt of your 7/13/2020, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), for records pertaining to changes to the agency's "Return Policy" as well as 1. Statistics on facilitation of return requests (also called FoRR Inquiries) from 1/1/09 to present including the following: 

a. Number of requests received for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017. 
b. Number of requests granted for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017. 
c. Number of requests denied for facilitation of return per year for 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, and 2017.  (see request for additional details).  Your request was received in this office on 7/13/2020. 

By letter, dated 7/14/2020, we advised you that due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we could encounter some delay in processing your request. We sincerely apologize for the delay and any inconvenience it may have caused. 

Before we begin the time-consuming review process, we want to ensure that you are still interested in continuing the processing of this request. Please indicate your continued interest in pursuing these records within 30 days from the date of this letter, or we will assume you are no longer interested in this FOIA request, and the case will be administratively closed. 

Your request has been assigned reference number 2020-ICFO-63645. Please refer to this number any future correspondence. If you have any questions please contact FOIA Public Liaison, Daniel Edgington at (866) 633-1182 or 500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009, Washington, D.C. 20536-5009. 

Sincerely, 

ICE FOIA Office 
Immigration and Customs Enforcement 
Freedom of Information Act Office 
500 12th Street, S.W., Stop 5009 
Washington, D.C. 20536-5009

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2020-ICFO-63645
communications.communication:
Yes, even though it's been over 4 years, we are still requesting these documents. They should be produced immediately, as the statutory deadline has long since passed. Thank you. 

Matthew Hoppock

title: Request to FBI for 2017 budget for Office of Public Affairs

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to FBI for 2017 budget for Office of Public Affairs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The 2017 annual budget for the Office of Public Affairs of the Federal Bureau of Investigation.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: CTS - Delaware

communications.subject: Delaware Freedom of Information Act Request: CTS - Delaware
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Delaware Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of each CTS Individual Report that your forensics laboratory received from Collaborative Testing Services (CTS) for CTS Test No. 23-5262 (Firearms Examination). CTS is a company that creates and distributes proficiency tests for forensic laboratories. It sends standardized test samples to labs and then provides reports showing how each examiner performed. The "Individual Report" is the completed assessment, which includes the examiner's answers.
I am requesting the individual report for each examiner who took the test at your agency. For instance, if several examiners took CTS Test No. 23-5262, I would like a copy of each examiner's report.
Additionally, I am requesting this report for every forensic lab in your agency. If your agency has multiple locations or satellite labs, I need the test results for examiners at those sites too.
CTS provides this report directly to the laboratory through the CTS portal and by email when the test cycle closes.
To aid you in your search, I recommend contacting your laboratory’s designated Lab Account Owner (Primary Contact) with CTS. CTS assigns each lab a Primary Contact when the lab account is created, and this person is the individual to whom CTS ships test materials and sends all reports. The Primary Contact has default access to the CTS portal features that contain the Individual Reports. The Primary Contact also receives an emailed pdf copy of the individual report.
Please provide the report in electronic format. Please acknowledge receipt of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg, a citizen of Delaware, in coordination with Sachi McClendon.

title: DoD Congressional Correspondence re: USS Liberty
tags: israeli attack on the uss liberty

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: DoD Congressional Correspondence re: USS Liberty
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I was aboard the USS Liberty when the IDF attacked our ship on June 8, 1967 and serve my shipmates as Historian of the USS Liberty Veterans Association.

In the attached extract from the “Department of Defense Office of the Secretary of Defense Congressional Correspondence 2008 to 2011” there is a reference highlighted by a red rectangle.

We request all the correspondence and documents related to that reference.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joe Meadors

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #25-F-3178
communications.communication:
In the referenced document there is a line that says:

“SUBJECT: CONCERNS REGARDING USS LIBERTY VETERANS FILED A FORMAL REPORT WITH DOD”

The formal report referenced is “Report:War Crimes Committed Against U.S. Military Personnel, June 8, 1967” we filed with the Department of Defense on June 8, 1967. [See https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/22135684-wcr-with-exhibits/]

Since the War Crimes Report was filed in on June 8, 2005, the beginning date for your search should be 6/8/2005.

title: Kennesaw Flock

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Kennesaw Flock
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request concerns Flock Safety cameras (including automated license plate reader / ALPR devices) within the City of Kennesaw, including records held by the Kennesaw Police Department, for the period from the inception of the City’s relationship with Flock (or the earliest date responsive records exist) through the present. All statutory references below are to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq.

Definitions and Instructions (please apply throughout):

a) “Record” is used here as defined in O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70(b)(2), which defines “public record” to mean all documents, papers, letters, maps, books, tapes, photographs, computer based or generated information, data, data fields, or similar material prepared and maintained or received by an agency or by a private person or entity in the performance of a service or function for or on behalf of an agency. Consistent with that definition, this request covers responsive records in any format, including without limitation emails, text/SMS messages, messaging-app communications (e.g., Microsoft Teams, Slack), voicemails, calendar entries, drafts, metadata, attachments, and embedded files. Because the statutory definition turns on a record’s function rather than the device or account on which it resides, this request also covers responsive records concerning City business held on personal accounts or devices.

b) “Flock” means Flock Safety, Flock Group Inc., and any parent, subsidiary, affiliate, or authorized reseller; and “the cameras” / “the system” means any automated or fixed/mobile license plate reader (ALPR/LPR), camera, sensor, or associated software or network supplied, hosted, operated, or serviced by the foregoing, regardless of the trade or brand name used.

c) Scope of custodians: this request applies to responsive records held by any City of Kennesaw department or official, including but not limited to the Police Department, the Office of the Mayor and City Council, the City Manager, Finance/Procurement, Legal, and Information Technology.

d) Vendor-held records: pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70(b)(2), records maintained by Flock or any vendor on the City’s behalf are public records subject to disclosure to the same extent as if held by the City. Please obtain and produce responsive vendor-held records accordingly.

e) Format: please produce records in their native electronic format with metadata intact; do not convert searchable electronic records into image-only files. Availability of any data on a public website does not satisfy a request for that data in its native electronic format.

Records Requested:

1) All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, statements of work, purchase orders, amendments, and renewals with Flock.

2) All invoices, payment records, and budget documents identifying the funding source, including any grant applications or awards.

3) All records identifying who authorized the deployment — City Council agendas, minutes, votes, resolutions, and any internal memoranda or staff reports.

4) All communications (including emails, text messages, and messaging-app messages, with attachments) between City or Police officials and Flock, and among City personnel, regarding acquisition, deployment, renewal, or use of the system.

5) Records sufficient to show the number and physical location of every installed camera.

6) All policies, standard operating procedures, and access/usage policies governing the cameras and the collected data.

7) The data retention schedule and policy.

8) All data-sharing agreements and records identifying every outside entity (other agencies, state or federal partners, the national Flock network) with access to the data.

9) Existing audit logs and search/query logs reflecting use of the system, in the format the system already maintains or can export. I am not asking the City to create a new compilation; I request the standard logs/exports as they exist.

10) Records identifying the sources of any “hot lists” used by the system.

11) Any privacy impact assessment, civil-liberties review, or community-notification records.

12) All training materials provided to staff regarding the system.

Procedural Requests:

– If any category is voluminous and cannot be produced within three business days, please provide the statutorily required description, timeline, and cost estimate rather than a denial.

– For any record or portion withheld or redacted, please notify me of the specific legal authority for withholding by Code section, subsection, and paragraph, as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(d) (the exemptions being enumerated in O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72), and produce all reasonably segregable non-exempt material.

– If you locate no responsive records for any numbered item, please state which custodians and systems were searched and the search terms used.

– Please use the most economical means reasonably calculated to identify and produce responsive records, as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(c)(1).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

QS Intelligence Group

title: Ashland Police (OR) Axon ALPR records

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of the following public records, which were recently produced under a previous public records request APD2025-441. 

-Policy 430 ALPR.pdf
-APD_Axon Fleet Contract.pdf

In the previous request APD2025-441, the City of Ashland was asked for:
 (1) all policies CITY/COUNTY NAME has that oversee and/or regulate the operation and use of Automated License Plate Readers, including the operation and use of Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares (2) a copy of any and all invoices CITY/COUNTY NAME has between any and all Automated License Plate Readers softwares and programs, including Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares (3) a map of all ALPR cameras that CITY/COUNTY NAME owns, operates or has access to (4) a copy of any and all license and user agreements, contracts, memorandums of understanding or other similar type documents that describe and/or regulate the use, retention, collection, and sharing of ALPR data including but not limited to the agreements between Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares 

It is my understanding that the City does not possess any additional records related to the above request, and therefore, the only responsive records were Policy 430 ALPR.pdf and APD_Axon Fleet Contract.pdf. However, if you have additional records request to (1), (2), (3), or (4), please produce those as well.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Southern Oregon Flock Off

title: Public Records Request — Minneapolis Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Public Records Request — Minneapolis Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Data Practices Responsible Authority
City of Minneapolis
350 S. Fifth Street, Room 301
Minneapolis, MN 55415

Dear Data Practices Responsible Authority,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to the City of Minneapolis's use of generative AI tools by city employees, including Microsoft Copilot.

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, Minn. Stat. § 13.03, we are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by City of Minneapolis employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing City of Minneapolis employee use of Microsoft Copilot or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to city employees, including the Minneapolis Police Department.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) has deployed AI assistants to more than 22,000 state employees and approved Microsoft Copilot Chat as a tool for handling non-public data within the Microsoft Government Community Cloud (GCC) environment (see MNIT, "2025 Annual Report," https://mn.gov/mnit/about-mnit/legislative-reports/2025-annual-report.jsp; see also MnDOT, "Generative Artificial Intelligence Standards," https://www.dot.state.mn.us/policy/it-data/it-003.html). The City of Minneapolis, including the Minneapolis Police Department, has reportedly generated over 37,000 items using Copilot. Minnesota police departments have also adopted AI-powered report writing tools to generate police reports from body-worn camera audio (see MPR News, "An AI tool is generating police reports for some Minnesota departments, prompting legal concerns," Oct. 8, 2025, https://www.mprnews.org/story/2025/10/08/minnesota-police-departments-using-ai-to-generate-reports). A League of Minnesota Cities publication encouraged city workers to use tools like Copilot and ChatGPT to jumpstart drafts and complete tasks more efficiently (see League of Minnesota Cities, "Shaping the Future of AI in Your City," https://www.lmc.org/news-publications/magazine/may-jun-2024/ai-in-your-city/).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Microsoft Copilot Chat interaction data within the Microsoft 365 GCC environment may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. Copilot usage reports and activity data can be accessed via the Microsoft 365 admin center under Reports > Usage. For ChatGPT, conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. The Minnesota Government Data Practices Act covers all "government data," defined as all data collected, created, received, maintained, or disseminated by a government entity regardless of physical form. Minn. Stat. § 13.02, subd. 7. Governor Walz's Executive Order 24-03 (2024) directed state agencies to develop policies for responsible AI use including transparency and accountability. Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) has issued guidance requiring that AI-generated content used in official business be treated as government data subject to existing retention schedules. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Public Records Request — Minneapolis Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Hello,
The City of Minneapolis accepts requests for data through our online portal here: https://minneapolis.service-now.com/opencityportal
Once you register you can submit your request using specific forms to more accurately route your request, check on the status of your request, and receive your responsive data all within the portal.
If you would prefer not to use the portal you can call 311  (612-673-3000) and they will submit a request on your behalf. You will still need to provide contact information or set up a way for us to provide you with the data.
You can also request your report at the Public Service Building in downtown Minneapolis.
If you have any questions, or experience any difficulties using the portal, please let us know.
More information about data practices, city policy, and other frequently asked questions is available here: http://www.minneapolismn.gov/datapractices/index.htm


Katie Knudsen l Enterprise Information Management Analyst l City of Minneapolis – Office of City Clerk l Katherine.Knudsen@minneapolismn.gov<mailto:Katherine.Knudsen@minneapolismn.gov>

communications.communication:
Hi 208986-04701536@requests.muckrock.com, Thank you for registering with OpenCity, the City of Minneapolis portal for public records requests. (https://minneapolis.service-now.com/opencityportal) Your user name: 208986-04701536@requests.muckrock.com 
 If you need to reset your password, please go to OpenCity and click the “Forgot Password” link in the top right corner to be emailed a new password. Learn About Data Practices Our OpenCity portal includes an Knowledge Base with articles about how to submit an effective data request, answers to frequently asked questions, and information about data practices at the City. (https://minneapolis.service-now.com/opencityportal?id=kb_view2) 
 Available Data To explore data already published online from the City of Minneapolis, please visit the Minneapolis Open Government web page. (http://www.minneapolismn.gov/government/open/index.htm) 
 Creating a New Request Now that you have registered, you can log into the OpenCity portal to: 
 submit a request for data. check the status of your requests. communicate with the Data Practices team about a specific request. receive responsive data directly through the portal. 
 Please do not reply to this email.

communications.subject: OpenCity data request DR26_005787 submitted to the City of Minneapolis
communications.communication:
Hi 208986-04701536@requests.muckrock.com, 
Thank you for submitting a data request with the City of Minneapolis. Your reference number: DR26_005787 Please refer to this number in any correspondence related to your request. You may reply to this email to add comments to your request. 
 How do I get updates about my request? We will provide updates by email. In addition, you can view the status updates of your requests on the OpenCity portal. First login, then click on My Requests to check on the status of your request, communicate with the Data Practices team, and receive responsive data. (https://minneapolis.service-now.com/opencityportal) How long will my request take to be processed? The time it takes to process your request will vary depending on the complexity of your request, how many requests are currently in our review queue, and how many requests we're processing. You can view the most up to date information about requests in process at the Check Status page on the OpenCity portal. 4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to city employees, including the Minneapolis Police Department.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) has deployed AI assistants to more than 22,000 state employees and approved Microsoft Copilot Chat as a tool for handling non-public data within the Microsoft Government Community Cloud (GCC) environment (see MNIT, "2025 Annual Report," https://mn.gov/mnit/about-mnit/legislative-reports/2025-annual-report.jsp; see also MnDOT, "Generative Artificial Intelligence Standards," https://www.dot.state.mn.us/policy/it-data/it-003.html) The City of Minneapolis, including the Minneapolis Police Department, has reportedly generated over 37,000 items using Copilot. Minnesota police departments have also adopted AI-powered report writing tools to generate police reports from body-worn camera audio (see MPR News, "An AI tool is generating police reports for some Minnesota departments, prompting legal concerns," Oct. 8, 2025, https://www.mprnews.org/story/2025/10/08/minnesota-police-departments-using-ai-to-generate-reports) A League of Minnesota Cities publication encouraged city workers to use tools like Copilot and ChatGPT to jumpstart drafts and complete tasks more efficiently (see League of Minnesota Cities, "Shaping the Future of AI in Your City," https://www.lmc.org/news-publications/magazine/may-jun-2024/ai-in-your-city/)

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Microsoft Copilot Chat interaction data within the Microsoft 365 GCC environment may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. Copilot usage reports and activity data can be accessed via the Microsoft 365 admin center under Reports > Usage. For ChatGPT, conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. The Minnesota Government Data Practices Act covers all "government data," defined as all data collected, created, received, maintained, or disseminated by a government entity regardless of physical form. Minn. Stat. § 13.02, subd. 7. Governor Walz's Executive Order 24-03 (2024) directed state agencies to develop policies for responsible AI use including transparency and accountability. Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) has issued guidance requiring that AI-generated content used in official business be treated as government data subject to existing retention schedules. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2276019/

To Date: 2026-04-28

title: school theatre

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: school theatre
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Public reports show that this school district has been subject to theatrical plays, musicals, and student performances: 
https://www.onstageblog.com/editorials/2024/11/16/school-district-cancels-high-school-play-after-obsencity-complaints

Therefore, I request the following: 
1) All instructional materials, presentations and presentation materials (including recorded video and audio, PowerPoint files, prepared remarks, and slides formats), and other guidance related to “Dog Sees God: Confessions of a Teenage Blockhead,” “parent complaints,” or obscenity. This includes A) any notes taken during meetings that discuss plays, musicals, or student performances B) any other document that has been used to help explain plays, musicals, and student performances, including internal documentation, public relations materials, and executive summaries.
2) Any digital communications (including but not limited to emails and text messages) as well as documents, (including but not limited to PDF, word processing, excel, and slide documents) about, pertaining to, or mentioning “Dog Sees God”, “parent complaints”, “Peanuts”, “obscenity”, or “offensive”.
3) Any emails, presentations, videos, memos, recordings, or other records mentioning theatrical plays, musicals, and student performances.
Please perform this search for records between Jan 1, 2024 and the time this request is processed. This request is not for commercial purposes and will be used in the course of reporting for 404 Media, an independent, journalist-owned news site (www.404media.co).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Koebler

title: ALPR/Flock Safety Records

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: ALPR/Flock Safety Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Data Retention Policies: Any policies, standard operating procedures (SOPs), or retention schedules specifying how long captured license plate data, images, and metadata are stored by the agency before deletion.

2. Data Sharing Agreements: A list, log, ledger, or copy of active Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs) detailing all outside agencies, law enforcement entities, or third parties with whom the Peachtree City Police Department shares its Flock Safety / ALPR data.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Buchanan

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

All emails to/from Trista Harper from March 5, 2025 through March 8, 2025. 

Please search all CPS email accounts as well as personal accounts used for public business. The log should include but not be limited to: sender, recipient(s), date and time, subject line, and attachment.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019538-050526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
June 5, 2026 Miriam Bhimani 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019538-050526 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: This letter is in reply to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request seeking the following information: All emails to/from Trista Harper from March 5, 2025 through March 8, 2025. RESPONSE: Records responsive to your FOIA request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center. Please visit the link and enter your username and password to access the records. http://cps.edu/About_CPS/Departments/Law/Pages/FOIARequest.aspx .. The safety and security of students and staff are of the utmost importance and the records have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(v):Vulnerability assessments, security measures, and response policies or plans that are designed to identify, prevent, or respond to potential attacks upon a community’s population or systems, facilities, or installations, the destruction or contamination of which would constitute a clear and present danger to the health or safety of the community, but only to the extent that disclosure could reasonably be expected to jeopardize the effectiveness of the measures or the safety of the personnel who implement them or the public. Information exempt under this item may include such things as details pertaining to the mobilization or deployment of personnel or equipment, to the operations of communication systems or protocols, or to tactical operations. Records have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7 (1)(f) exempts any “Preliminary drafts, notes, recommendations, memoranda and other records in which opinions are expressed, or policies or actions are formulated, except that a specific record or relevant portion of a record shall not be exempt when the record is publicly cited and identified by the head of the public body.” Please be advised that student information is exempt under FOIA and records have been redacted pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, section 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). Google meeting links have been redacted as they are exempt pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(u): Information that would disclose or might lead to the disclosure of secret or confidential information, codes, algorithms, programs, or private keys intended to be used to create electronic or digital signatures under the Electronic Commerce Security Act. Redactions have been made pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7 (1)(b), “Private information, unless disclosure is required by another provision of this Act, a State or federal law or a court order,” and  5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c), “Personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subjects of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs the legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. The disclosure of information that bears on the public duties of public employees and officials shall not be considered an invasion of personal privacy. Illinois state law prohibits the release of portions of public employee personnel files, such as teacher performance evaluations. Per 105 ILCS 5/24A-7.1, “Disclosure of public school teacher, principal, and superintendent performance evaluations is prohibited,” and 820 ILCS 40/11, “Disclosure of performance evaluations under the Freedom of Information Act shall be prohibited,” and related documents have been redacted. Section 7(1)(d)(iv) of the FOIA allows a public to withhold records/information that would unavoidably reveal the identity of a complainant or witness. Lastly, pursuant to Section 7.5(h) of the FOIA, exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). To the extent you consider this a denial, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a) you may file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at: Public Access Counselor; Office of the Attorney General; 500 South 2nd Street; Springfield, IL 62706.  You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court; 5 ILCS 140/11.  Thank you for your request. Sincerely, Ana Diaz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Flock Email Requests (Capitola Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Email Requests (Capitola Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide all records of email communications, including attachments and calendar invitations, that were sent to or received from the domain “@flocksafety.com,” between November 1, 2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.

This request includes:
- Emails where @flocksafety.com appears in the sender, recipient (To, CC, BCC), or within the body of the message
- Attachments of any file type, including but not limited to PDFs, presentations, spreadsheets, and images
- Calendar invitations, meeting requests, and related responses involving Flock Safety personnel

Please search all agency-controlled email systems and accounts reasonably likely to contain responsive records, including individual officer accounts, group mailboxes, and archived or backup systems. Records should be provided in native electronic format where possible (e.g., .pst, .msg, or .eml with metadata preserved), including all available metadata fields such as sender, recipients, timestamps, and subject lines.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: Request for Public Records # 26-124
communications.communication:
The City of Capitola ("City") is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on April 20, 2026 ("Request 26-124").

The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.). Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requester makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort.  The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request.

The City has reviewed its records inventory and identified records responsive to your request. The City is reviewing records for responsiveness and privilege.  Review is ongoing.  The City will endeavor to provide responsive records on or before May 14, 2026.  If you have any questions regarding the City's response or would like to discuss your Request, please email me.

Sincerely,

Julia Gautho, CMC
City Clerk
jgautho@ci.capitola.ca.us<mailto:jgautho@ci.capitola.ca.us>
(831) 475-7300 x. 228

You had assigned it reference number #26-124.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request #26-124
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

The City of Capitola (“City”) is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on April 20, 2026 (“Request”), seeking “all records of email communications, including attachments and calendar invitations, that were sent to or received from the domain ‘@flocksafety.com,’ between November 1, 2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.”

The City wishes to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act. Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requester makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The Public Records Act provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to create new records, lists, privilege logs, or reports in response to a request.

On April 30, 2026, the City determined that it possesses records responsive to your Request.  The City now provides the initial batch of records responsive to your Request through the following link: https://bwslaw.sharefile.com/d-s9153de22c0ad4fa6950417c37938bf15

The City withheld any responsive law enforcement records pursuant to Government Code section 7923.600.  The City also withheld certain records pursuant to California Civil Code section 1798.90.55(b) (See also Cal. Gov. Code § 7927.705.).  The City redacted camera locations as the public interest in withholding clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure to prevent camera tampering or vandalism and because the cameras are law enforcement investigatory tools (Cal. Gov. Code section 7922.000).

The City is in the process of reviewing records for responsiveness and privilege. The City will endeavor to provide an additional production of responsive records on or before May 28, 2026.  If you have any questions regarding the City’s response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact me at cityclerk@ci.capitola.ca.us.


[cid:image001.jpg@01DCE3BE.88438510]
Julia Gautho, CMC
City Clerk
City of Capitola   |  420 Capitola Ave.  |  Capitola CA 95010
(831) 475-7300  |   jgautho@cityofcapitola.gov<mailto:jgautho@cityofcapitola.gov>|  cityofcapitola.gov <http://www.cityofcapitola.gov/>
Follow us online: @CityofCapitola

communications.subject: Public Record Request #26-124
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,
The City of Capitola (“City”) is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on April 20, 2026 (“Request”), seeking “all records of email communications, including attachments and calendar invitations, that were sent to or received from the domain ‘@flocksafety.com,’ between November 1, 2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.”
The City wishes to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act. Under established California law, the City of obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requester makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The Public Records Act provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to create new records, lists, privilege logs, or reports in response to a request.
On April 30, 2026, the City determined that it possesses records responsive to your Request. On May 14, 2026, the City provided the first batch of records responsive to your Request. The City now provides the second batch of records responsive to your Request through the following link:
https://bwslaw.sharefile.com/public/share/webs7d5e379090154dbe9372bef15ab3b9c6
The City redacted camera locations as the public interest in withholding clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure to prevent camera tampering or vandalism and because the cameras are law enforcement investigatory tools. (Cal. Gov. Code section 7922.000.) The City redacted personal contact information as the public interest served in withholding personal contact information clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure, to prevent an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. (Cal. Gov. Code section 7922.000; See also: Cal. Const., art. 1 § 1.).
The City withheld certain confidential financial information pursuant to Government Code section 7922.000. The City also withheld records that are draft documents not maintained in the ordinary course of business and/or that may reveal deliberative process of the City staff. Cal. Government Code §§ 7927.500, 7922.000; Citizens for a Better Environment v. California Department of Food and Agriculture (1985) 171 Cal.App.3d 704; The Humane Society of the United States v. Superior Court of Yolo County (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 1233.
With this letter and production, the City considers your Request closed. If you have any questions regarding the City’s response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact me at cityclerk@cityofcapitola.gov
Sincerely,


Rosie Wyatt

Deputy City Clerk

831.475.7300 x 206

title: Northern Idaho/Eastern Washington Flock Safety User Group

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Northern Idaho/Eastern Washington Flock Safety User Group
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Regarding the Northern Idaho/Eastern Washington Flock Safety User Group which has previously hosted events at the Spokane County Sheriff's RTCC:
1. Recordings, documents, presentations (such as PowerPoint, Google slides, etc), training materials, memos, and other materials from the User Group meetings provided to the Sheriff's Office
2. Communications with Flock Safety since January 1, 2025 regarding the User Group

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Sheriff Public Records Request :: S026758-123025
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Spokane County. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 12/30/2025 and given the reference number S026758-123025 for tracking purposes. Spokane County Sheriff provides access to public records in compliance with applicable laws and statutes of the State of Washington. In accordance with the public records law, the Sheriff's office has five business days to respond to a request for public records. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Regarding the Northern Idaho/Eastern Washington Flock Safety User Group which has previously hosted events at the Spokane County Sheriff's RTCC: 
1. Recordings, documents, presentations (such as PowerPoint, Google slides, etc), training materials, memos, and other materials from the User Group meetings provided to the Sheriff's Office 
2. Communications with Flock Safety since January 1, 2025 regarding the User Group 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2167344/ 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
 
Please be advised that Spokane Regional Emergency Communications ("SREC") is the custodian of 911 and Crime Check audio recordings as well as audio recordings of dispatch and radio traffic. If you are attempting to obtain audio recordings of 911/Crime Check calls and/or radio traffic, you need to submit a separate request to SREC directly. You can submit a records request to SREC via their website, https://www.srec911.com. 
Spokane County

Rose Terse: 
In accordance with the Public Records Act, RCW 42.56.520, this message acknowledges receipt of your public records request, received by Spokane County on 12/30/2025. This request has been assigned reference codeS026758-123025. Please include this code in any communications concerning this request. 
Specifically, you requested: “To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Regarding the Northern Idaho/Eastern Washington Flock Safety User Group which has previously hosted events at the Spokane County Sheriff's RTCC: 
1. Recordings, documents, presentations (such as PowerPoint, Google slides, etc), training materials, memos, and other materials from the User Group meetings provided to the Sheriff's Office 
2. Communications with Flock Safety since January 1, 2025 regarding the User Group 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2167344/” 
Our procedure is to locate records, review them for exemptions, and then prepare them for your inspection or copying. Due to the potential volume of records that may be responsive to this request, as well as the complexity of identifying and gathering the records, we anticipate processing this request in installments. We are currently processing over 1292 active requests. This does mean that it can take time to process requests, but we work to provide requestors with installments of their records on a timely basis. 
We reasonably anticipate an installment/requested records will be available on or before May 1, 2026. If public records become available in advance of this estimate, we will let you know. Conversely, if staff encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will advise accordingly. 
With respect to audio recordings, please be advised that Spokane Regional Emergency Communications ("SREC") is the custodian of 911/Crime Check audio recordings as well as audio recordings of radio traffic. If you are attempting to obtain audio recordings of 911/Crime Check calls and/or radio traffic, you need to submit a separate request to SREC directly via the following link: https://srec911.com/ 
Finally, please be advised that there may be fees associated with the copying of public records. Please take a moment to  familiarize yourself with Spokane County's Public Records Request Policy and Fee Schedule, which can be found in the FAQs section. 
Sincerely, 
JOHN F. NOWELS, Sheriff 
Spokane County

communications.communication:

Rose Terse: 
This message is to provide an update on your public records request assigned reference code S026758-123025. 
With regard to Item 1 of your request, no records responsive to your request were located. This portion of your request is now considered concluded and closed because no responsive records were located. 
With regard to Item 2 of your request, we conducted a Teams search and an email search for all SCSO staff from January 1, 2025, to December 30, 2025. Based on our understanding of your request based on the language of your request, we used the search term "Flock Safety User Group". The responsive records located by this search are now available for download in the “My Request Center” of the public records portal. If we have misunderstood your request or the search terms you desired, please provide clarification within 30 days or by 06/01/2026. If no clarification is received from you by that date, we will consider your request fulfilled, and it will be closed as completed. 
The Spokane County Sheriff's Office is obligated to provide records in accordance with the Washington State Public Records Act. Please note that the records you have requested may contain images and/or descriptions of events that are considered graphic in nature and may not be suitable for children or those who are sensitive to such material. 
Please claim your installment by reviewing or downloading the available records. If this installment has not been claimed within 30 days, or by 06/01/2026, this request will be considered abandoned and will be administratively closed as of that date. Once the available installment has been claimed, we will provide you with the estimated date of the next installment. To schedule an appointment to view the records in person at no charge, please call us at 509-477-6926. 
Sincerely, 
JOHN F. NOWELS, Sheriff 
Spokane County

Public Records Specialist
